rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEVENS VS. JOHN MILLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To A Request For The Production Of Documents - Set Two; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Defts
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 25, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JOHN STEVENS Motion To Compel Further Responses To A Request For The Production Of Documents - Set Two; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants. Pro Tem Judge Peter VanZandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff John Stevens' Motion to Compel Further Responses to A Request for Production of Documents and for Sanctions is GRANTED. Defendants Touraj Boussina and Perry Boussina are ORDERED to fully and completely respond to Plaintiff Stevens' Document Request No. 20 seeking profit and loss statements related to the operation of the bar located at 3231 Fillmore Street, San Francisco, California for the time period the bar was owned by Defendants Touraj Boussina and Perry Boussina. The profit and loss statements of this business are not "constitutionally protected" as Defendants suppose. Defendants' refusal to turn over financial records from the very business the Plaintiff claims to have lost his investment and who is seeking damages on a cross complaint is an unreasonable abuse of the discovery process. Defendants Touraj Boussina and Perry Boussina are hereby ORDERED to serve an original, verified, complete response, without objection to Plaintiff Stevens' Document Request No. 20 seeking profit and loss statements related to the operation of the bar located at 3231 Fillmore Street, San Francisco, California for the time period the bar was owned by Defendants Touraj Boussina and Perry Boussina and pay sanctions in the amount of $750 to Lipton & Piper, LP within 15 days of the service of Notice of Entry of the Order arising from this hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: AARON HASHIM et al VS. JOHN CHIANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 25, 2014, Line 16, DEFENDANT JOHN CHIANG'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the Second Cause of Action for Violation of 42 U.S.C. sec. 1983 is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Qualified immunity shields Mr. Chiang from liability as an individual. "Government officials performing discretionary functions generally are shielded from liability for civil damages insofar as their conduct does not violate clearly established statutory or constitutional rights of which a reasonable person would have known." (Harlow v. Fitzgerald (1982) 457 U.S. 800, 818.) The Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") pleads that Mr. Chiang complied with the provisions of the UPL. (SAC, pars. 15, 20.) The Ninth Circuit upheld the new escheatment statutory scheme as Constitutional on its face. (Taylor v. Westly (9th Cir. 2008) 525 F.3d 1288, 1289 ("Taylor III").) Because the Complaint does not allege conduct outside the parameters of the escheatment statutes, a reasonable person would not have known that his conduct would violate Constitutional rights. Although the section 1983 action against Mr. Chiang individually fails, Plaintiff may be able to amend the SAC to state a cause of action against the State directly to the extent it seeks damages equal to the amount of the property held in trust only or an injunction. (See Taylor v. Westly (9th Cir. 2005) 402 F.3d 924, 932-35 ("Taylor I"); see also Suever v. Connell (9th Cir. 2009) 579 F.3d 1047, 1059, "Our case law states that the Eleventh Amendment does not bar claims by plaintiffs for the return of their own property under the UPL, because such claims are not for 'damages' against the State.") The demurrer to the third cause of action for Violation of CCP secs. 1300 et seq. is sustained without leave to amend. Although the goal of the UPL is to reunite escheated property with its owner, there is a specific statute, Section 1530(b)(1), providing that identifying information does not have to be collected for property valued at less than $50. (SAC, par. 15.) When a conflict exists between a general and a specific law, the provisions of the specific law prevail. (CCP § 1859; see also e.g. People v. Superior Court (2002) 28 Cal.4th 798, 808.) The Complaint pleads that Mr. Chiang complied with Section 1530(b)(1). (SAC, par. 20.) The demurrer to the first cause of action for Declaratory Relief is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. There may be a basis for a declaratory relief cause of action, but its contours would have to be consistent with the ruling on the demurrers to the other causes of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD A DENTON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Ntc Of Mo & Mo To Compel Deft, City And County Of San Francisco To Respond To Demand For Production Of Electronically Stored Information (Esi) To Provide Esi In "Native Format"
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 25, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF RICHARD DENTON MOTION / Motion To Compel Defendant, City And County Of San Francisco To Respond To Demand For Production Of Electronically Stored Information (Esi) To Provide Esi In "Native Format". (Part 1 of 2). Pro Tem Judge Peter VanZandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Counsel for Plaintiff are admonished to read and comply with local rules. Courtesy copies of the moving papers were not provided to Department 302, a violation of Local Rule 2.6. Plaintiff RICHARD DENTON'S Motion to Compel Defendant City and County of San Francisco ("CCSF") to Respond to Plaintiff's Demand for Production of Electronically Stored Information ("ESI") in Native Format is GRANTED. Unfortunately, the moving papers and supporting declarations presented by both parties are of limited use and guidance to the court. CCSF did not provide a compelling explanation for refusing to provide the documents in their native format which would include relevant metadata. In turn, Plaintiff's lay person declaration makes broad, sweeping generalizations without any factual support. "Without metadata it is impossible to confirm the authenticity of any email." [Decl. of Denton in Support of Reply 2:25-26] The declaration of Brendan Patrick in support of CCSF's Opposition to the motion is equally unpersuasive, providing no logical technological reason for CCSF refusing to produce the ESI in native format. The fact that the City Attorney's office does not use Outlook and counsel cannot review email documents in that format is of no moment. Their client, CCSF does use Outlook and they must produce documents whether the format is specified or not "¿in the form or forms in which it is ordinarily maintained¿" (Code of Civil Procedure Section 2031.280(c)(d)(1)). Repackaging the information for the convenience of counsel is an inexplicably impoverished explanation. Nowhere in the Opposition does CCSF articulate with any specificity any undue burden or relevance argument upon which the court could tailor a more restrictive order based on a balancing of the relative importance of the information against the hardship entailed by its production. (Borse v. Superior Court (1970) 7 Cal. App. 3d 286, 289). CCSF is ORDERED to produce all responsive, non privileged documents in their native format to Plaintiff within 15 days of the service of Notice of Entry of the Order arising from the hearing on this matter. (Continued to Part 2)

rulings.Case Title: KEN LAM et al VS. CALIFORNIA CAPITAL INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 25, 2014, Line 14, DEFENDANTS WANG INSURANCE AGENCY, INC., SIMON GIANG'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion denied. Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint (FAC) sufficiently alleges a basis for an award of attorneys' fees. (See Prentice v. North Amer. Title Guar. Corp (1963) 59 Cal.2d 618, 620-21.) The FAC alleges causes of action against the Insurance Defendants for breach of contract and reformation, which Plaintiff has been required to pursue in order to protect his interests as a result of Defendants' alleged negligence, which is a sufficient basis for seeking relief pursuant to the tort of another doctrine. (See id.) Furthermore, the relief sought is not precluded merely because Plaintiff also asserts claims for negligence, fraud, and misrepresentation against the Insurance Defendants, nor due to the allegations that all defendants may be liable as joint tortfeasors. It is well established in California that "a party may plead in the alternative and may make inconsistent allegations." (Third Eye Blind, Inc. v. Near North Entertainment Ins. Services, LLC (2005) 127 Cal.App.4th 1311, 1323.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT HALF INTERNATIONAL, INC. A DELAWARE CORPOR VS. DONNA FARRUGIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 25, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANTS DONNA FARRUGIA, BRIAN GABRIELSON, GARY D NELSON ASSOCIATES INC., D/B/A NELSON FAMILY OF COMPANIES' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Continued on the Court's motion to December 26, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LITTLER MENDELSON, P.C. VS. DUKE CAPITAL PARTNERS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 25, 2014, Line 11, PLAINTIFF LITTLER MENDELSON, P.C.'S Motion To Amend Judgment. The motion is granted in part. Plaintiff has leave to amend to name Duke Capital Partners, LLC in place of Duke Capital Partners, Inc. The court denies the remainder of the motion as withdrawn. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN MOORE ET AL VS. CAMERON R. BRYCE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 25, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANTS CAMERON BRYCE, BRYCE CB CONSTRUCTION INC.'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. The motion is denied. Defendant relies solely on the declaration of counsel (Mr. Anderson) to obtain relief. That declaration fails to show that the default was obtained due to mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: VADIM RYABOY VS. COASTAL TRADE SECURITIES, LLC A DELAWARE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 25, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANTS MARC PRESTON, CHRISTOPHER APOLITO, COASTAL TRADE SECURITIES, LLC, SASHA PRESTON'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons. The motion is granted. Plaintiff's service of the summons and complaint did not comply with CCP 412.30 or 416.10(b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: WENDY HEMMING ET AL VS. YETIZEN INNOVATION LAB LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 25, 2014, Line 17, PLAINTIFFS WENDY HEMMING, 55 NATOMA, LLC, A WASHINGTON CORPORATION'S APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER.
